objective: Calibrate cheap deterministic structural metrics against the exact broken-connection, silhouette-collapse, and articulation-drift controls without inferring visual quality or owner judgement.
next_action: Audit the existing reference and three negative-control snapshots, derive a minimal orthogonal integer metric matrix bound to the H3 candidate and exact scene fingerprints, then add in-memory adversarial fixtures for missing controls, cross-sensitivity, stale bindings, threshold drift, and overclaim before any integrated gate.
research_gate: Use the existing Forge-owned reference viewport, H3 receipt, and typed control snapshots. No external assets, human reference, renderer, engine, or runtime research is required for H4.
authority_lane: Owner-authorized implementation of the approved master plan. H4 may calibrate deterministic structural metrics only; it cannot assert asset quality, human anatomy, perception, preference, approval, promotion, engine compatibility, or protected-Kernel mutation.
context_health: Green after H3 focused and complete Forge gates. P13-P15 stage-quality enforcement is active; capture is current and heartbeat remains paused.

macro_findings: H4 calibrates functional controls after H3 generation and before H5 visual observation.
F5 remains engine-neutral; G1 production and R1 runtime work remain gated.
Metric discrimination cannot establish a good-quality human, perceptual quality, player preference, approval, or promotion.
micro_findings: The existing controls are deterministic and distinct: one removes the right knee-ankle link, one narrows frame-zero front span, and one changes frame-one hand height while preserving segment length.
Edge deficit, rest front-span loss, and frame-one hand vertical displacement can form an orthogonal integer metric matrix without rendering pixels.
Every metric and control must bind exact reference/control fingerprints and declare non-detections rather than imply general quality.

visual_quality_gate.rationale: H4 reads typed point, edge, and frame coordinates and computes integer metrics in memory. It does not use a rendered image or human visual asset. H5 visual comparison remains separately gated by P13.

tiers_completed.result: Three existing control mutations affect independent structural dimensions that can be measured with exact integer differences.

simulation_ladder.final_integration_gate: After typed and in-memory H4 calibration passes, retain modularity and the complete Forge gate before H4 closure.

unresolved_risks: A metric may respond to more than its named control and be falsely described as specific.
A threshold chosen after viewing controls would encode post-hoc acceptance rather than calibration.
Stale reference or control fingerprints could silently rebind old evidence.
Structural metric separation could be overclaimed as visual or anatomical quality.
The H2/H3 wire fixture remains not a verified good-quality visual human.
evidence_requirements: exact H3 candidate and reference/control fingerprint bindings
integer-only metric definitions with no learned or post-hoc threshold
complete metric-by-control detection and non-detection matrix
adversarial missing-control, stale-binding, cross-sensitivity, threshold-drift, and overclaim fixtures
verification_plan: inspect exact reference/control mutations and existing stimulus bindings
derive the smallest orthogonal typed metric model
run in-memory reference and adversarial fixtures
freeze calibration fingerprints and record claim limits
run modularity, focused, and complete Forge gates
advance automatically to H5 only after H4 is verified
resume_after: Continue automatically after progress updates. Ask the owner only when H5 presents a genuine visual-quality, accuracy, anatomy, or creative-intent uncertainty.
verification_receipts: H3 fixed input and candidate fingerprints retained
transition: R0 accepted -> H1 intake verified -> H2 representation verified -> H3 generation verified -> H4 functional controls executing
